# Contributing

Thank you for your interest! 🎉
We welcome contributions of all kinds, including bug reports, feature requests, documentation improvements, and code enhancements.

## 🛠️ How to Contribute

### 1. Fork the Repository
Click the "Fork" button at the top right of this page to create your own copy of the repository.

### 2. Clone Your Fork
```
git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/github-content-publisher.git  
cd github-content-publisher
```

### 3. Create a New Branch
`git checkout -b feature/my-new-feature`

### 4. Make Your Changes
Make your changes in your local repository. Ensure that your code adheres to the project's coding standards and includes appropriate tests. 

### 5. Updating Documentation
Update the README.md or other documentation files if your changes affect how users interact with the project.

### 6. Push and Create a Pull Request
`git push origin feature/my-new-feature`

Then go to GitHub and open a Pull Request.

## 🚫 Tag Creation Policy
To ensure consistency and control over published versions:

- Tag creation is restricted to maintainers and release managers.
- Contributors must not push tags directly.

- ✅ Maintainer Workflow
Tags like v1.0.0, v1.1.0, etc., will trigger automated validation and prepare the module for publishing.

## ✅ Guidelines

- Follow code best practices.
- Keep code clean and well-documented.
- Add tests for new features.
- Update the README if needed.
- Be respectful and collaborative.

We appreciate your help in making this module better for everyone!
